zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Gedge於1546年6月22日出生在英國英格蘭艾可申費爾德時，她的父親James Gedge30歲，母親Margaret Barfield26歲。她於1557年2月3日與Edward Elliot在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 瑪麗 勒 包福 安德 聖 潘可拉斯 索 萊恩 煙雨 哥吉拉 哈洛福斯 宏尼 萊恩結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和5個女兒的父母。她去世於1595年8月22日在英國英格蘭肯特可雷福德享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Gedge于1546年6月22日出生在英国英格兰艾可申費爾德时，她的父亲James Gedge30岁，母亲Margaret Barfield26岁。她于1557年2月3日与Edward Elliot在英国英格兰倫敦聖 瑪麗 勒 包福 安德 聖 潘可拉斯 索 萊恩 煙雨 哥吉拉 哈洛福斯 宏尼 萊恩结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和5个女儿的父母。她去世于1595年8月22日在英国英格兰肯特可雷福德享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Gedge
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English gegge, a derogatory term used for both men and women, though the present pronunciation, rhyming with ledge, is unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Gedge was born on 22 June 1546, in Shenfield, Essex, England, United Kingdom, her father, James Gedge, was 30 and her mother, Margaret Barfield, was 26. She married Edward Elliot on 3 February 1557, in St Mary le Bow and St Pancras Soper Lane with All Hallows Honey Lane, London, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 5 daughters. She died on 22 August 1595, in Crayford, Kent, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Gedge est née le 22 juin 1546, à Shenfield, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, James Gedge, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margaret Barfield, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Edward Elliot le 3 février 1557, à St Mary le Bow and St Pancras Soper Lane with All Hallows Honey Lane, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 5 filles. Elle est décédée le 22 août 1595, à Crayford, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Gedge(이)가 1546년 6월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 셰필드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 James Gedge(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Barfield(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 1557년 2월 3일 영국 잉글랜드 런던 스트 메리 일 보 앤드 스트 파크라스 소퍼 랜 위스 앨 핼로스 호니 랜에서 Edward Elliot(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1595년 8월 22일 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 크라이퍼드에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Gedge родилась 22 июня 1546 г. в местности шенфиелд, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени James Gedge было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Barfield, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Edward Elliot 3 февраля 1557 г. в местности ст мэри л боу анд ст панкрас сопир лан вит алл холлоус хоуни лан, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 5 дочерей. Она умерла 22 августа 1595 г. в местности креифорд, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.
